observation: A dense, irregular mass with smooth, diffuse boundaries is present, showing continuous internal texture without defined edges or faceting; the morphology is consistent with phase separation or amorphous aggregation rather than crystalline growth.

Dense granular structures are present within part of the drop, forming a clustered region without clearly defined edges; the appearance is consistent with amorphous precipitate but could overlap with microcrystalline features.
The drop appears clear with no visible particles, precipitate, or crystal-like structures.
A single well-defined crystal with sharp, straight edges is visible near the edge of the drop, while the rest of the drop remains clear and free of amorphous material.
Multiple well-defined geometric crystals with sharp edges are visible, separated and clearly distinguishable from the background.
